calendar.matters:
ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar Nov-28-2017 Granted Per Written Order
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ronald Wargo appeared by telephone for Edward Donahue.

The Court noted that there are two issues: 1) purchase of the property at substantially less than the appraised value and 2) the need for a revised order with an omnibus clause. Mr. Wargo stated that the single property is worth $5,000.00 at most and maybe less now since the fires, that they have already spent $3,400.00 in costs, that the cost to identify heirs would exceed $5,000.00 and that if required to do so, his Client is willing to dismiss the petition. Mr. Wargo also stated that they did a 10301, that no one bid on it, that the Decedent was survived by five children and all five of them are now deceased. The Court stated that it will then go to their estates. The First and Final Account and Report of Administrator, etc. is taken under submission. Counsel is to submit a revised order.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: D. Johnson. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION

ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: ACCOUNTING #1, REPORT, FEES, FINAL DISTRIBUTION
Off Calendar May-09-2017 Continued To Jul-18-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ronald Wargo appeared by telephone for Edward Donahue (Petitioner).

Mr. Wargo stated that the property has not officially been sold, that they have not done the deed of transfer and that he would like a Court order. Mr. Wargo also stated that thinks it is not a 1963 appraisal, that he thinks it's the current value and will be getting confirmation, that if not, the Administrator will pay the increase in value and asked for a 45-day continuance. The Court informed Counsel that the 9202(b) certification needs to be corrected. The Verified First and Final Account and Report of Administration, etc. is continued to July 18, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204 for Counsel to get everything done. If the matter is not in a position to go forward, Counsel will need to file a petition for continued administration.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: A. Jaime. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION
Off Calendar Jan-11-2017 Continued To Feb-27-17 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES:

Appearances: Ronald Wargo for Edward Donahue.

Mr. Wargo stated that the Estate will close with the purchase of a $1,500.00 piece of property and that he could wrap it up within a month. The Petition to Approve First Status Report of Administrator is continued to February 27, 2017 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204.

Judge: John K. Stewart. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Deputy: K. Wu. Not reported.

PROBATE ACCOUNTING STATUS PETITION

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Re Closing Of Estate
Off Calendar Nov-15-2016 Off Calendar

ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR Status Re Closing Of Estate
Off Calendar Oct-26-2016 Continued To Nov-15-16 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Ronald P. Wargo, for petitioner Edward S. Donahue.

Mr. Wargo stated that there was one small property to be sold prior to closing of the estate, which would probably take another 60-90 days. The Court responded that if the estate was not in a position to close, a petition to approve status report should be filed and set for hearing. Mr. Wargo stated that he would do so. The matter was continued to November 15, 2016 at 9:00 a.m. in Dept. 204 for s status hearing regarding the filing of the petition to approve status report. The Court noted that if the petition was on file prior to that date, the status hearing would go off calendar.

Judge: Peter J. Busch. Clerk: Craig Blackstone. Bailiff: Deputy Rosado. (Not reported)
